audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar scouting-of-Canaan text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for scout commission, tribal representatives, renaming of Hoshea/Joshua, land and produce report, fortified cities, Caleb's minority report, and fear rhetoric about Anakim/Nephilim. Strong anti-colonial, anti-racist, and anti-dehumanization safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Send for yourself men, and let them scout land of Canaan, which I am giving to children of Israel; one man, one man from each tribe of his fathers you shall send, every one a leader among them.
Moses sent them from wilderness of Paran at mouth/command of YHWH; all of them were men, heads of children of Israel.
These were their names: from tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur;
from tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori;
from tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;
from tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph;
from tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Nun;
from tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu;
from tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi;
from tribe of Joseph, from tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi son of Susi;
from tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli;
from tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael;
from tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi;
from tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.
These are names of men Moses sent to scout land; Moses called Hoshea son of Nun, Joshua/Yehoshua.
Moses sent them to scout land of Canaan and said to them, Go up this way through Negev and go up into hill country.
See what land is, and people dwelling upon it, whether strong or weak, whether few or many;
and what land is in which they dwell, whether good or bad; and what cities are in which they dwell, whether camps or fortifications;
and what land is, whether fat or lean, whether there are trees in it or not. Strengthen yourselves and take from fruit of land. Now days were days of first-ripe grapes.
They went up and scouted land from wilderness of Zin to Rehob, toward entrance of Hamath.
They went up through Negev and came to Hebron, and there were Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, descendants of Anak. Hebron had been built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.
They came to wadi of Eshcol and cut from there a branch with one cluster of grapes, and they carried it on a pole by two, with pomegranates and figs.
That place was called wadi Eshcol/Cluster because of cluster children of Israel cut from there.
They returned from scouting land at end of forty days.
They went and came to Moses and Aaron and all congregation of children of Israel, to wilderness of Paran at Kadesh; they brought back word to them and all congregation and showed them fruit of land.
They recounted to him and said, We came to land where you sent us; indeed it flows with milk and honey, and this is its fruit.
Nevertheless, people dwelling in land are strong, cities fortified and very great; moreover descendants of Anak we saw there.
Amalek dwells in land of Negev; Hittite, Jebusite, and Amorite dwell in hill country; and Canaanite dwells by sea and along side of Jordan.
Caleb quieted people before Moses and said, Surely we should go up and possess it, for surely we are able to prevail over it.
But men who went up with him said, We are not able to go up against people, for they are stronger than we.

They brought out an evil report of land that they had scouted to children of Israel, saying, Land through which we passed to scout it is a land eating its inhabitants, and all people whom we saw in its midst are men of great measure.
There we saw Nephilim—sons of Anak are from Nephilim—and we were in our own eyes like grasshoppers, and so we were in their eyes.

translation_notes: The scouting mission belongs to an ancient conquest narrative. It must not be used to justify modern colonization, ethnic cleansing, land seizure, or displacement.
The inhabitants are named peoples with homes and cities; they must not be flattened into obstacles or racialized monsters.
Verse 16 preserves Hoshea's renaming as Joshua/Yehoshua.
The report moves from observable facts—fruit, population, fortifications—to fear-laden interpretation.
Nephilim and Anakim language is ancient giant/legendary-warrior rhetoric and requires lexical and reception-history audit.
'We were like grasshoppers' is the scouts' self-perception and projected assumption about others, not an objective statement of human worth.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 13 to sanctify conquest, colonial expansion, racism, forced displacement, or dehumanization of indigenous peoples. Preserve the ancient narrative while distinguishing observation from fear-amplified rhetoric and refusing modern territorial weaponization.
